steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: State the rectangle area formula
steps.content: The area of a rectangle equals length times width.

steps.title: Apply the formula to the given dimensions
steps.content:
With length = 7 inches and width = 6 inches:
$$A = 7 \times 6 = 42 \text{ square inches}$$
The units become square inches because two lengths are multiplied. That confirms C is correct.
